pr.title: PR #124 â€” GitHub Metadata Import Framework v1
pr.description:
## Summary

Adds the **GitHub Metadata Import Framework v1**: deterministic, read-only ingestion of structured GitHub PR metadata into shapes consumable by the Global Capability Registry (#122) and the Capability Event Timeline (#123). It replaces manual data entry with metadata ingestion while preserving raw payloads, schema versioning, and manual override capability. No network/GitHub API, no GitHub mutation, no orchestration.

Input is a metadata payload (a fixture file â€” no network). The engine parses PR/commit/file/label metadata, then **builds (never mutates)** a derived registry entry and lifecycle timeline events:

```
import_metadata(metadata, repo=None, pr_number=None, global_capability_number=None):
    pr = GitHubPRMetadata.from_dict(...)        # manual overrides win over payload
    require owner / name / pr_number            # else -> ValueError "Malformed PR metadata"
    reject if (owner, name, pr_number) already imported   # -> "Duplicate import"
    commits sorted by (timestamp, commit_sha)   # malformed (no sha) skipped -> PARTIAL_IMPORT
    files   sorted by (path)                     # malformed (no path) skipped -> PARTIAL_IMPORT
    labels  sorted by (name)                     # malformed (no name) skipped -> PARTIAL_IMPORT
    registry_entry  = GlobalCapabilityEntry(... status via _PR_STATUS_TO_REGISTRY_STATUS ...)
    timeline_events = [PR_CREATED@created_at, PR_READY@updated_at, PR_MERGED@merged_at]
                      # only emitted when the timestamp is present; sorted by timeline key
    status = PARTIAL_IMPORT if skipped else IMPORTED
    persist report.json + 4 evidence files
```

**Pass/fail:** `passed = (status == IMPORTED)` â€” any skipped sub-record flips the import to `PARTIAL_IMPORT` and `passed: false`.

**Status enum:** `IMPORTED` / `PARTIAL_IMPORT` / `FAILED`.

## What changed

- `src/axiom_core/github_metadata_import.py` (new): models `GitHubPRMetadata` / `GitHubCommitMetadata` / `GitHubFileChangeMetadata` / `GitHubLabelMetadata` / `GitHubMetadataImport` / `GitHubMetadataImportReport` / `GitHubMetadataImportEvidence` + `GitHubMetadataImportEngine` (consumes the registry + timeline dataclasses). Path-sandboxed evidence under `artifacts/github_metadata_import/<run_id>/`; JSON/Markdown/CSV export.
- `src/axiom_cli/main.py`: `axiom github-import` (`--metadata-file`, `--repo`, `--pr`, `--global-capability-number`, `--json-output`), `github-import-show`, `github-import-export`. Example: `axiom github-import --repo owner/name --pr 10 --global-capability-number 124 --metadata-file <f>`.
- Registered in `command_registry.py`, `test_selection_engine.py`, `tests/test_command_registry.py`.
- `tests/test_github_metadata_import.py` (new): models, deterministic ordering, registry population, PR_CREATED/READY/MERGED event creation, manual overrides, duplicate rejection, malformed (partial) handling, exports, evidence bundle, pass/fail, path safety.

## What did NOT change

No architecture changes, no broad refactoring, no new dependencies, no network calls, no GitHub mutation, no Devin import, no orchestration. Revit 2024 baseline unaffected. Existing registry/timeline artifacts are never mutated â€” the framework only produces new derived shapes within its own evidence bundle.

## Validation

- `poetry run pytest -q` â†’ **4023 passed, 1 skipped** (+48; baseline 3975 at #123).
- `poetry run ruff check src/ tests/` â†’ **0 errors**.
- CLI smoke: import (IMPORTED, deterministic commit/file/label ordering), duplicate import â†’ exit 1, `github-import-show missing` â†’ exit 2, `github-import-export ../../etc` â†’ path-safety exit 1, all 3 export formats verified.

## Risks

Low. Self-contained new module + additive CLI commands; no changes to existing runtime behavior. No Revit live validation required; 2024 baseline unaffected.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
